title: AI's Bottleneck is Physical
summary: AI does not become useful when a model is trained. It becomes useful when a physical feedback loop — power, heat, latency, sensors, data lineage, safe actuation — can run reliably. Two interactive figures trace the loop and show why capability is not deployability.
feedDescription: The next bottleneck in AI is physical: whether the loop from electricity through inference to actuation and back to sensors can run reliably, efficiently, and observably.

title: Reading CausalPFN: causal effects as in-context prediction
summary: A NeurIPS 2025 paper trains one transformer on simulated, identifiable causal worlds; new observational data goes in as context, treatment-effect posteriors come out. What holds it up, and where it breaks.
feedDescription: Notes on Balazadeh et al., CausalPFN: one transformer, trained on simulated identifiable DGPs, that estimates treatment effects in-context.

title: The geometry of forgetting in continual learning
summary: How learning a new task damages an old one—and how interference geometry suggests a structural controller.
feedDescription: Forgetting as interference energy under a task's feature covariance, and the IGFA controller.

title: Solving the electrode calendering U-shape puzzle
summary: Why particle reorientation reverses the monotone prediction made by porosity-only thermal models.
feedDescription: Why through-plane conductivity dips before it rises, and the closure that captures it.
